act: Protection of Children from Sexual Offences Act 2012 (POCSO)
description: Special law to protect children from sexual abuse and exploitation, with child-friendly procedures.

sections.title: Penetrative Sexual Assault on Child
sections.text: A person is said to commit penetrative sexual assault if he commits penetrative sexual assault on a child who is under 18 years of age.
sections.simplified: Any sexual assault on a child (under 18 years) is a serious crime under POCSO Act. The law treats children as special victims and provides enhanced punishments.
sections.punishment: Minimum 10 years, extendable to imprisonment for life, and fine

sections.title: Sexual Assault on Child
sections.text: Whoever, with sexual intent touches the vagina, penis, anus or breast of the child or makes the child touch the vagina, penis, anus or breast of such person or any other person, or does any other act with sexual intent which involves physical contact without penetration, is said to commit sexual assault.
sections.simplified: Any inappropriate touching of a child with sexual intent is a crime under POCSO, even if it doesn't involve penetration. This includes making the child touch someone else inappropriately.
sections.punishment: Imprisonment of 3 to 5 years, and fine

sections.title: Sexual Harassment of Child
sections.text: A person is said to commit sexual harassment upon a child when such person: (i) utters any word or sound or makes any gesture intended to be seen or heard by the child for sexual purpose, (ii) makes a child exhibit his body, (iii) shows any object or media in any form for sexual purpose, (iv) repeatedly follows or contacts the child, (v) threatens to use sexual imagery of the child, (vi) entices the child for pornographic purposes.
sections.simplified: Sexual harassment of a child includes: showing sexual content to a child, stalking a child, making sexual comments, threatening to share sexual images of the child, or trying to lure a child for pornography. All are crimes.
sections.punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years and fine

sections.title: Reporting of Offences
sections.text: Any person who has apprehension that an offence under this Act is likely to be committed or has knowledge that such an offence has been committed, shall provide such information to the Special Juvenile Police Unit or the local police. Failure to report is punishable.
sections.simplified: ANYONE who knows about child sexual abuse MUST report it to the police or Special Juvenile Police Unit. Not reporting is itself a crime. You can also call CHILDLINE at 1098.
sections.punishment: Failure to report: imprisonment up to 6 months and/or fine

sections.title: Special Court Procedures for Children
sections.text: The evidence of the child shall be recorded within 30 days. The child shall not be called repeatedly to testify. The child's identity shall not be disclosed. The Special Court shall ensure the child is not exposed to the accused during testimony.
sections.simplified: POCSO provides child-friendly trial procedures: the child's statement is recorded within 30 days, the child doesn't have to face the accused during testimony, the child's identity is kept confidential, and the trial must be completed within 1 year.

sections.title: Aggravated Penetrative Sexual Assault
sections.text: Whoever commits penetrative sexual assault on a child is said to commit aggravated penetrative sexual assault if the offender is a police officer, armed forces member, public servant, teacher, guardian, or relative of the child.
sections.simplified: If the abuser is a family member, teacher, police officer, or any person in a position of trust or authority, the crime is treated as 'aggravated' and carries even harsher punishment — minimum 20 years to life imprisonment.
sections.punishment: Minimum 20 years, extendable to imprisonment for life or death, and fine
